summary: A well-structured, clearly organized test suite that covers every stated requirement across 7 explicit TDD cycles: main/master→latest, PR tags, semver (with and without v-prefix, prerelease vs stable aliases), feature-branch SHA tags, sanitization (uppercase, special chars, collapsed hyphens), error handling, and integration scenarios. Deduplication and the sanitize_tag helper are tested directly. Design is excellent — descriptive names, docstrings, logical grouping, no shared mutable state, and the TDD-cycle comments make progression easy to follow. Rigor is strong but not perfect: missing tests for (1) a feature branch with an empty/short commit SHA, (2) a branch composed entirely of special characters that would sanitize to empty, (3) semver build metadata (+build suffix), (4) invalid/negative PR numbers, and (5) a commit SHA shorter than 7 chars. These omissions represent plausible real-world inputs that could expose edge-case bugs, keeping rigor at 4 rather than 5.
